app_callbacks.h(3) Library Functions Manual app_callbacks.h(3)

NAME

app_callbacks.h - Application-level callbacks for library code.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct app_callbacks_t

Macros


#define APP_CALLBACK_VOID(callback_name)
#define APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(callback_name)
#define APP_CALLBACK_INT(callback_name)
#define APP_CALLBACK_INT_SOCKET(callback_name, sockfd)
#define APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT8(callback_name, value)
#define APP_CALLBACK_VOID_STR(callback_name, value)
#define APP_CALLBACK_VOID_INT(callback_name, value)
#define APP_CALLBACK_PTR(callback_name)

Functions


void app_callbacks_register (const app_callbacks_t *callbacks)
const app_callbacks_t * app_callbacks_get (void)

Detailed Description

Application-level callbacks for library code.

Allows lib/ code to call application-level functions without direct dependency.

Definition in file app_callbacks.h.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define APP_CALLBACK_BOOL( callback_name)

Value:

({                                                                                                                   \


  const app_callbacks_t *__cb = app_callbacks_get();                                                                 \


  (__cb && __cb->callback_name) ? __cb->callback_name() : false;                                                     \
})


Call a void(void) callback and return bool Usage: bool result =
    APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)


Definition at line 65 of file app_callbacks.h.


65 #define APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(callback_name)                                                                               \
66   ({                                                                                                                   \
67     const app_callbacks_t *__cb = app_callbacks_get();                                                                 \
68     (__cb && __cb->callback_name) ? __cb->callback_name() : false;                                                     \
69   })






Referenced by connection_attempt_tcp(),
    connection_attempt_websocket(), and
  session_client_like_run().






#define
  APP_CALLBACK_INT( callback_name)


Value:


({                                                                                                                   \


  const app_callbacks_t *__cb = app_callbacks_get();                                                                 \


  (__cb && __cb->callback_name) ? __cb->callback_name() : -1;                                                        \
})




Call a void(void) callback and return int Usage: int result =
    APP_CALLBACK_INT(client_crypto_init)


Definition at line 75 of file app_callbacks.h.


75 #define APP_CALLBACK_INT(callback_name)                                                                                \
76   ({                                                                                                                   \
77     const app_callbacks_t *__cb = app_callbacks_get();                                                                 \
78     (__cb && __cb->callback_name) ? __cb->callback_name() : -1;                                                        \
79   })






Referenced by connection_attempt_tcp(), and
    connection_attempt_websocket().






#define
  APP_CALLBACK_INT_SOCKET( callback_name, sockfd)


Value:


({                                                                                                                   \


  const app_callbacks_t *__cb = app_callbacks_get();                                                                 \


  (__cb && __cb->callback_name) ? __cb->callback_name(sockfd) : -1;                                                  \
})




Call a int(socket_t) callback Usage: int result =
    APP_CALLBACK_INT_SOCKET(client_crypto_handshake, sockfd)


Definition at line 85 of file app_callbacks.h.


85 #define APP_CALLBACK_INT_SOCKET(callback_name, sockfd)                                                                 \
86   ({                                                                                                                   \
87     const app_callbacks_t *__cb = app_callbacks_get();                                                                 \
88     (__cb && __cb->callback_name) ? __cb->callback_name(sockfd) : -1;                                                  \
89   })






Referenced by connection_attempt_tcp().






#define
  APP_CALLBACK_PTR( callback_name)


Value:


({                                                                                                                   \


  const app_callbacks_t *__cb = app_callbacks_get();                                                                 \


  (__cb && __cb->callback_name) ? __cb->callback_name() : NULL;                                                      \
})




Get a pointer return value from callback Usage: const
    crypto_context_t *ctx =
    APP_CALLBACK_PTR(crypto_client_get_context)


Definition at line 131 of file app_callbacks.h.


131 #define APP_CALLBACK_PTR(callback_name)                                                                                \
132   ({                                                                                                                   \
133     const app_callbacks_t *__cb = app_callbacks_get();                                                                 \
134     (__cb && __cb->callback_name) ? __cb->callback_name() : NULL;                                                      \
135   })






Referenced by connection_attempt_tcp(), and
    connection_attempt_websocket().






#define
  APP_CALLBACK_VOID( callback_name)


Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  const app_callbacks_t *__cb = app_callbacks_get();                                                                 \


  if (__cb && __cb->callback_name) {                                                                                 \


    __cb->callback_name();                                                                                           \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Call a void(void) callback safely Usage:
    APP_CALLBACK_VOID(audio_stop_thread)


Definition at line 53 of file app_callbacks.h.


53 #define APP_CALLBACK_VOID(callback_name)                                                                               \
54   do {                                                                                                                 \
55     const app_callbacks_t *__cb = app_callbacks_get();                                                                 \
56     if (__cb && __cb->callback_name) {                                                                                 \
57       __cb->callback_name();                                                                                           \
58     }                                                                                                                  \
59   } while (0)






Referenced by session_client_like_run(), and
    session_render_loop().






#define
  APP_CALLBACK_VOID_INT( callback_name, value)


Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  const app_callbacks_t *__cb = app_callbacks_get();                                                                 \


  if (__cb && __cb->callback_name) {                                                                                 \


    __cb->callback_name(value);                                                                                      \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Call a void(int) callback Usage:
    APP_CALLBACK_VOID_INT(server_connection_set_port, port)

Function
  Documentation




const
  app_callbacks_t * app_callbacks_get (void )


Get registered callbacks (returns NULL if not registered)


Definition at line 15 of file app_callbacks.c.

16   return g_app_callbacks;
17 }

  app_callbacks_register (const app_callbacks_t * callbacks)
